text: I want you to just close your eyes for a second.
Imagine you are standing in a field, but it is not a green field.
The ground is hard like concrete.

And in the middle of this completely dry, dead landscape, there's a large rock.
And sitting on that rock is a woman.
That is a very rough, scratchy fabric.
It is the clothing of mourning, the clothing of just deep, deep sadness.
She is sitting there, but she is not resting at all.
Maybe she is holding a branch or a stick.
That is the only word for it.
She is fighting the birds, the vultures, the big black birds that eat dead things.
They are trying to land on the rock.
She swings her stick at them.
She flat out refuses to let them land.
And this goes on all day.
But then she hears something else in the darkness.
She hears the jackals, the wild dogs, the beasts of the field.
They are coming out of the shadows to eat.
And so this woman stands up in the dark, maybe with a torch, maybe just with her voice,
and she fights them off too.
She does this for days, she does this for weeks, she does this for months.
It is, it is honestly one of the most powerful, heartbreaking images in the entire Bible.
